name: preconsistent
statement_text:
A linear multistep method satisfying (404a) is said to be
‘preconsistent’.

  Now consider the differential equation

                             y  (x) = 1,        y(x0 ) = 0,

with exact solution at the step values

                                        yi = hi.

If this solution has been found for i = n − k, n − k + 1, . . . , n − 1, then it is
also correct for i = n if and only if
                                                                               
 nh = α1 (n − 1)h + α2 (n − 2)h + · · · + αk (n − k)h + h β0 + β1 + · · · + βk .

Assuming the method is preconsistent, the factor h can be cancelled and then
n times (404a) can be subtracted. We then find

                   α1 + 2α2 + · · · + kαk = β0 + β1 + · · · + βk .                (404b)

This leads to the following definition:
statement_latex:
\begin{definition}
A linear multistep method satisfying \eqref{eq:404a} is said to be `preconsistent'.

Now consider the differential equation
\[
y'(x) = 1, \qquad y(x_0) = 0,
\]
with exact solution at the step values
\[
y_i = hi.
\]
If this solution has been found for $i = n - k, n - k + 1, \dots, n - 1$, then it is also correct for $i = n$ if and only if
\[
nh = \alpha_1 (n-1)h + \alpha_2 (n-2)h + \cdots + \alpha_k (n-k)h + h \bigl( \beta_0 + \beta_1 + \cdots + \beta_k \bigr).
\]
Assuming the method is preconsistent, the factor $h$ can be cancelled and then $n$ times \eqref{eq:404a} can be subtracted. We then find
\[
\alpha_1 + 2\alpha_2 + \cdots + k\alpha_k = \beta_0 + \beta_1 + \cdots + \beta_k. \tag{404b}
\]
This leads to the following definition:
\end{definition}

variables.name: α_i
variables.definition: coefficients for past solution values in the linear multistep method

variables.name: β_i
variables.definition: coefficients for past derivative values in the linear multistep method

variables.name: k
variables.definition: number of steps in the linear multistep method

variables.name: y_n
variables.definition: numerical approximation to the solution at step n

variables.name: f
variables.definition: function defining the ODE: y'(x) = f(x, y(x))

preamble: The context discusses linear multistep methods for ODEs, defined by coefficients α_i and β_i. Preconsistency is a property ensuring exact solution for a trivial ODE, leading to condition (404a).
